steps.text:
You come to on the sawdust floor. A blank mask looks down at you.

"A valiant effort from our challenger, but no dice!" the Ringmaster says. "We do hope you enjoyed the show."

You retreat from the stage...
The crowd erupts. The Ringmaster's voice cuts through the applause: "It seems our challenger has triumphed this time! Will they be so lucky when they return?"

You push through the entrance flap and step into another world.

Lanterns hang in clusters from every post. Performers weave between clusters of onlookers — a fire-breather, a contortionist, a man swallowing a sword with practiced boredom. The smells of popcorn and animal musk compete for dominance.

You find the entrance flap. Through the canvas, the plateau air reaches you — cold and still.

Something steps in front of it.

The figure is tall, dressed in a tailcoat the colour of deep red wine. A white mask with a carved smile. A top hat, slightly askew. He holds a scepter loosely in one hand.

You didn't see him arrive.

The Ringmaster tilts his head. When he speaks, his voice fills the tent without being raised.

"Already? The show has only just begun."

You move toward the flap.

Something shifts between you and the exit. The Ringmaster stands there — still, unhurried — scepter raised across the gap like a bar across a cage.

"Our guests don't leave until the show is over." He tilts his head. "And the show is never over."

on_victory.text:
The Ringmaster watches you from behind the mask for a long moment.

Then, with a single precise movement, he steps aside.

"More trouble than you're worth." Something in his voice sounds almost amused. "Go, then. The show will go on without you."

steps.text: The House of Mirrors stands before you, as if taunting. You get a strange feeling as you stand at the entrance.

fight.text: The tent is full of mirrors. Every one shows you looking back — except one, which shows something else entirely.

The masked reflection steps forward, brandishing a knife.

You mirror it and prepare to fight!

on_victory.text:
The mirror shatters. The others show only empty glass now.

...

Creeped out, you leave the house of mirrors.

on_defeat.text:
You come to on the floor, just outside the house of mirrors.

...

...That was creepy.

steps.text: A spotlight snaps on above the main stage. The ringmaster's voice echoes from nowhere: "Ladies and gentlemen — it is time for the dance to begin!"

then.text: Two Harlequins vault onto the stage from either side, knives already drawn. The crowd holds its breath.

else.text: A Harlequin cartwheels onto the stage, knife in hand, porcelain mask obscuring their face.

steps.text: A spotlight snaps to you, and the ringmaster's voice speaks: "A challenger approaches! Will they step up and join the dance?"
